auth.networkError: Cannot reach the API. Is it running? Check the API is started and CORS allows this origin.
auth.companyIdRequired: Company ID is required. Your company must be set up by an administrator first.
auth.companyNotFound: No company found with this Company ID. The company must be created by an administrator first.
auth.userLimitReached: This company has reached its user account limit. Contact your company administrator.
auth.inviteDescription: Choose your user name and password. You will sign in as a company administrator.
auth.inviteMissingLink: This page needs the invitation link from your email. Open the link from the message you received.
auth.inviteInvalid: This invitation is invalid or has expired. Ask a Super Admin to resend it.
auth.forgotPasswordDescription: Enter your email and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
auth.resetLinkSent: If an account exists for this email, you will receive a reset link.
auth.resetSuccess: Password reset successfully. You can now sign in.
auth.productTourHint: Explore features before you sign in.

home.title: Home
home.signedIn: You are signed in. Bookings and more below.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.signedIn: You are signed in. Bookings and more below.

stats.title: Booking stats
stats.description: Your company's bookings by period and breakdown by courier and cities
stats.descriptionAll: All companies' bookings by period and breakdown by courier and cities
stats.heatmapLegendTapDay: Tap or click a day to see completed bookings and drafts.
stats.scopeHint: Charts and totals follow completed vs drafts

earnings.title: Platform earnings (EUR)
earnings.description: Payable totals from billing line items (EUR, UTC). Daily ranges use posting time; monthly ranges use calendar months. Excludes lines marked excluded from invoice.
earnings.noData: No billing data for this period.

cards.courierContractsDescription: Choose which couriers your company uses and add contract IDs

bookings.title: Bookings
bookings.comingSoon: Booking list will appear here when the API is ready.
bookings.saveToAddressBook: Save sender and receiver to address book after create
bookings.viewDraftDescription: View-only. You cannot edit or confirm this draft.
bookings.editDraftDescription: Fill in the rest and save. When ready, confirm to complete the booking. Required fields are checked only when you confirm.
bookings.quickBookingDescription: When enabled, only mandatory fields in each section are shown; optional fields are hidden.
bookings.validationActionBanner: Some required fields are missing. Scroll up to the highlighted errors, fix them, then try again.
bookings.trialLimitBannerHint: Upgrade your subscription or ask an administrator to assign a paid plan so you can keep creating bookings.

sections.shipperDescription: Sender party details. Select from address book or enter manually.
sections.receiverDescription: Receiver party details (required). Select from address book or enter manually.
sections.payerDescription: Optional payer for freight. Copy from sender/receiver or enter other.
sections.packagesDescription: Add one or more packages. Use "Add package" for multiple packages.

bookings.noCouriersBannerAdmin: Your company has no courier contracts yet. Add the carriers you use and your contract IDs before creating bookings.
bookings.noCouriersBannerUser: Your company has not configured courier contracts yet. Ask a company administrator to set them up.
bookings.importHint: Use the same column headers as exported bookings.
bookings.importMappingDescription: Your file headers differ from the export template. Choose which file column fills each booking field, or leave “No column” to keep that field empty.
bookings.importSavedMappingDescription: This file name and column layout match a mapping your company saved earlier.
bookings.importSavedMappingHint: You can apply that mapping as-is, or open the mapper to change it. Applying updates the saved mapping only if you check “Remember mapping” on the next screen.
bookings.importSaveMappingForCompany: Remember this mapping for this company (same file name and columns)
bookings.importDialogDescription: Confirm which rows to create. Empty rows in the file are skipped.
bookings.importCountCompleted: Completed bookings (will be created as finished): {count}
bookings.importCountDrafts: Incomplete rows (will be saved as drafts): {count}
bookings.importNoDataRows: No data rows found in this file.

actions.title: Actions
actions.description: Agreement numbers, default shipper, address book and other company-related actions.
actions.comingSoon: Coming soon when backend endpoints are available.
actions.addressBookDescription: Senders and receivers per company. Use these when creating bookings. Not shared between companies.
actions.noCompany: No company linked to your account. Contact an administrator.
actions.name: Name
actions.noSenders: No senders yet. Add one below.
actions.noReceivers: No receivers yet. Add one below.
actions.alreadyExists: This address already exists in the address book
actions.addToAddressBook: Save to address book after create
actions.bookingFieldRulesDescription: Choose whether each section and field is mandatory or optional for users creating bookings. Applies to everyone in your company. Validation runs in the portal only.
actions.rulesAdminOnly: Only company administrators can change booking requirements.

courierContracts.title: Courier contracts
courierContracts.description: Select which integrated couriers your company may use and enter the contract ID for each. All users in your company will only see these couriers when creating bookings.
courierContracts.superAdminNoAccess: Super administrators manage companies from Manage; courier contracts are configured by each company’s administrator.
courierContracts.readOnlyHint: Only company administrators can change courier contracts. Below is what is currently enabled for your company.
courierContracts.catalogUnavailable: Could not load the list of available couriers. Try again or contact support.

plugin.title: Plugin
plugin.description: Download the plugin and view release notes.
plugin.comingSoon: Plugin download and release notes will appear here when the API is ready.

more.title: More
more.courierContractsHint: Choose carriers and contract IDs for bookings.
more.subscriptionNonePlan: No subscription plan is assigned yet.
more.subscriptionTrialBookings: Up to {count} bookings included during the trial.
more.subscriptionRatesPending: Detailed rates will appear when configured.
more.subscriptionTierBandMax: Up to {max} bookings in the period

manageCompanies.editCompanyDescription: Update settings for {name}. Leave a field empty to keep its current value.
manageCompanies.validationNoChanges: Change at least one field or cancel.
manageCompanies.validationMaxUsersInvalid: Max users must be a valid whole number.
manageCompanies.validationMaxAdminsInvalid: Max admins must be a valid whole number.
manageCompanies.reductionDescription: Reducing how many users or admins this company may have requires demoting administrators and/or deactivating users. Demotions run first; selected accounts are then deactivated (they can no longer sign in).
manageCompanies.inviteEmailsEditHelp: Only while this company has no administrator. Updating emails revokes all unused pending invites, then you can check “Send invites” to mail the new list.
manageCompanies.inviteResendAfterSave: Send invitation emails now (uses the list above; revokes any unused pending invites first)
manageCompanies.inviteTooManyEmails: Number of emails cannot exceed max admins.

manageInvoices.title: Billing & invoices
manageInvoices.description: View subscription billing for a company over a UTC date range (first billable instant). Payable total excludes lines marked excluded from invoice. PDF, email, and per-booking exclusion apply only when the range falls in a single UTC calendar month.
manageInvoices.noPeriods: No billing periods yet for this company.
manageInvoices.emailRequiresBusinessId: This company has no business id on file; invoice email cannot verify the recipient.
manageInvoices.periodUtcHint: Dates are interpreted as UTC calendar days. The end date is inclusive.
manageInvoices.datesRequired: Choose a start and end date.
manageInvoices.endBeforeStart: End date must be on or after start date.
manageInvoices.noBookingsInRange: No billable bookings in this date range.
manageInvoices.multiMonthInvoiceHint: This range spans more than one UTC month. Totals and segments include all matching bookings; use a single-month range to download PDF, send email, or change exclusions.
manageInvoices.invoiceActionsSingleMonth: Invoice PDF, email, line detail, and exclusions are available when all bookings in the range fall in one UTC month.

manageReleaseNotes.title: Release notes email
manageReleaseNotes.description: Send a message to portal users. Formatting matches a typical email app: line breaks and spacing are preserved.
manageReleaseNotes.composeDescription: Choose who receives the message, then send. Only active users with an email address are included.
manageReleaseNotes.subject: Subject
manageReleaseNotes.resultPartial: Sent to {sent} of {total} recipients. Some failed:
manageReleaseNotes.smtpDescription: Sends a short test through the API SMTP settings (Smtp:Host, Smtp:FromAddress).
manageReleaseNotes.validationCompanies: Select at least one company or choose all companies.
manageReleaseNotes.validationRoles: Select at least one role or choose all roles.

manageSubscriptionPlans.title: Subscription plans
manageSubscriptionPlans.description: Create and maintain subscription templates, pricing periods, and tier rows.
manageSubscriptionPlans.name: Name
manageSubscriptionPlans.confirmDelete: Delete this plan? This cannot be undone if no companies use it.

tour.metaDescription: Multi-tenant logistics: bookings, APIs, bulk import, waybills, carriers, plans, invoicing, and platform administration.

hero.title: Operate freight with clarity
hero.subtitle: One hub for bookings, network insight, and carrier reality—scoped per company, governed when you run the platform.

platform.title: Built for ops, visibility, and control
platform.intro: Four capability layers map to how teams actually work—not a feature laundry list.
platform.card1Body: From booking to delivery visibility with fewer handoffs and one shared picture of what is moving.
platform.card2Body: Volume, carriers, routes, and signals that need attention—before they become customer escalations.
platform.card3Body: Desktop plugin path and release visibility so rollouts do not surprise your desk teams.
platform.card4Body: Companies see only their data; platform operators govern tenants, access, and plans.

bookings.title: Get every shipment documented and moving
bookings.body: One flow for parties, stops, and cargo. Reuse contacts, save progress, switch to essentials-only when speed matters.
bookings.b4: Waybill PDFs—generate and print when the shipment reaches the right milestone
bookings.bulkBody: Upload structured files to create multiple bookings in one pass—ideal for campaign drops, seasonal volume, or migrating lists from legacy tools.

dashboard.title: See performance before it becomes a fire drill
dashboard.body: Leadership views for what is busy, who carries your volume, and where friction shows up early.

workflow.title: Speed for the desk, clarity on change
workflow.body: Shortcuts for everyday operational tasks plus a path to stay current with what shipped in the product.

integrations.title: Plug in your stack—APIs & e-commerce
integrations.body: Connect your store, ERP, or custom services with CargoHub’s APIs—bookings and updates flow in automatically so operations stay aligned without re-keying.
integrations.apiBody: Create and update bookings from your own systems or storefronts; keep orders and shipments in sync with a stable integration surface.

carriers.title: Work with your real carrier agreements
carriers.body: Configure the carriers and identifiers your company actually uses so bookings line up with provider contracts.

admin.title: For operators running the whole tenant base
admin.body: Onboard companies, control who can sign in, handle billing and subscription plans, and publish what changed.

subscriptions.title: Subscription tiers and custom plans
subscriptions.intro: Match each company to the right model: packaged tiers, bespoke deals, and operator workflows for billing periods and invoice PDFs when terms or volume need a custom fit.
subscriptions.tiersBody: Plans with included booking allowances, stepped pricing, and clear overage rules—straightforward to sell, assign, and enforce across tenants.
subscriptions.customBody: When standard tiers are not enough, operators can define tailored plans: custom limits, billing periods, and pricing so strategic or high-volume accounts get the right agreement.
subscriptions.invoiceBody: Billing periods, earnings breakdowns, and print-ready invoice PDFs—operators review platform revenue and send clear statements per company.

start.title: Get started in minutes
start.step1: Your organization is created by a platform administrator.
start.step2: You register with your company ID or accept an admin invite.
start.step3: Configure carriers, then create and track bookings.
start.faq: Company ID is your government business identifier—it ties your account to the right tenant.

tour.previewDisclaimer: Sample data only—not connected to your company.
